title: Object 67
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:the meat is beautiful, right?}} __NOTOC__
I had been wandering the wretched halls of [[Level 1]] for several days now. Some parking garage it is. There aren't even any cars in the Backrooms! Or, at least, they aren't a very convenient mode of transportation. But nonetheless, the Backrooms had still chosen an infinite, empty parking garage as one of its first levels. Ironic, isn't it? Imagine how much use a complex like this would have in [[the Frontrooms]]. Why don't the two dimensions have a trade: [[Level 1]] in exchange for a McDonald's or something? I'm getting really hungry. I still have some [[Object 1|almond water]] left over, but I used up my supply of [[Object 6|moth jelly]] three days ago, and the last of my [[Object 46|sensitive candies]] ran out. If I don't find a base or an exit to another level soon, I'll starve to death. That was what I was thinking when I saw ''it''. It was a bubbling mass of frothy redness and oils and fats. but in that moment, that wasn't what I saw. no, it wasnt. all i saw was food the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? the meat is beautiful right? i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it i would eat it wouldnt you?
= Object 67: "Molten Meat" = {{Blurimage|file=MEAT..png|caption=A close-up photograph of molten meat.|hovertext=the meat is beautiful right?}} == Description == '''Object 67''', also known as '''molten meat''' or '''spam''',More precisely, the term ''molten meat'' refers to the object as a whole, while ''spam'' refers to its most common variant. resembles a sludgy, frothy red fluid, sometimes mixed with a thin, clear yellow liquid assumed to be oil or fat. The substance is opaque and viscous, with a consistency similar to that of basaltic lava, and occurs mainly in industrial levels of the Backrooms such as [[Level 2]], [[Level 3]] or [[Level 105]]. The froth present in molten meat is uniquely and anomalously dark in colour. Despite its fairly unremarkable temperature, the substance is known to be extremely corrosive toward non-environmental surfaces (i.e., surfaces which are not inherently a part of its level's environment), and operatives have found it complicated to procure a sample of the object for research. '''Molten meat''' exudes a gas, known as '''cruoric gas''', which causes wanderers who inhale it to begin feeling exceptionally hungry, and makes their intestinal walls unable to absorb nutrients from almost any food which they may consume. If the wanderer has not eaten for a while, which is all too common for lone roamers of the Backrooms, they are liable to starve and perish. Additionally, the gas works its way into the wanderer's brain and forces them to subconsciously seek out the molten meat. Upon setting eyes on '''molten meat''', the wanderer will become infatuated with it, and experience a terrible urge to devour it so as to satisfy their unslakeable hunger. If cruoric gas has been allowed to sufficiently penetrate the brain, it can also induce a form of synesthesia, which disorients the wanderer and ensures the only thing which remains distinct to their senses is the meat. Patients who have inhaled the gas and survived have reported feeling an intense attachment to the molten meat when they first encountered it, declaring that they "could think of the meat, only the meat and nothing but the meat".In the words of one Patricia Anglicia. (Such patients have explicitly described the object as being "meat", although they seem unable to provide an explanation for how they instinctively arrived at this notion.) Unfortunately for those who consume it, '''molten meat''' is immensely toxic to almost all [[:Category:Organisms|biological life]], not to mention its corrosive properties. In addition, the cruoric gas it emits can shut down a wanderer's lungs if inhaled in large quantities, and anyone who has been exposed to it is advised to seek immediate medical assistance. No wanderer nor [[Entity List|entity]] has ever been known to survive consumption of the object. == Composition == The composition of '''molten meat''' is widely speculation to comprise many components of actual red animal meat — protein, fat, water, collagen, myoglobin, etc. — rolled together and anomalously liquefied into a singular miry substance. This stems from the theory that the cruoric gas exuded by the fluid is produced through rapid evaporation and is thus made of the same substance; tests performed on the gasCruoric gas is significantly less corrosive than liquid spam, although it is still wholly capable of dissolving such substances as metal over the course of a day. have revealed trace amounts of the aforementioned components. Most of the gas, however, simply bears the chemical makeup of the [[Entity 6|Sakastan gases]] secreted by [[Entity 6|dullers]]. Both liquid and gas are known to be extremely flammable; lighting a match in the presence of '''molten meat''' or cruoric gas has produced incendiary results. The black froth observed in spam has been shown to contain immense concentrations of anomalous pathogens, some of which have yet escaped proper documentation; this black froth is notably absent in samples of foulfowl, although whether such pathogens are present in smaller amounts despite this is still a topic of debate. == Usage == Due to its highly hazardous qualities and corrosive nature, there are not many practical uses for '''molten meat'''. Certain [[groups]] have considered weaponising the object, but none have yet figured out a viable method to do so. The notable [[Party Crashers]]' prototype flamethrower design, the LcF-A4,"LcF" is an abbreviation of French ''lance-flammes''. "A" refers to French ''anormal'' or English "anomalous". attempts to utilise canned ichthyin as an incendiary ammunition; however, due to ichthyin's inability to be contained by anything except its naturally occurring can, coupled with its overall rarity and the danger of the curoric gas it emits, the design is not fully practical as of yet. Using a [[Object 19|portable purifier]] to filter '''molten meat''' typically causes the object to solidify and become a chunk of raw meat, free of any pathogens due to the purifier's effects. However, this is unlikely to be practical due to cruoric gas's neurological effects. == Location == As aforementioned, '''molten meat''' is commonly found in industrially themed levels of the Backrooms. Typically, specimens of spam or foulfowl are encountered oozing out of strangely placed orifices in walls and ceilings, and is often mistaken for blood at a distance. The source of the molten meat is currently unknown; attempts to discover where these orifices lead have only led to the unearthing of rusted metal pipes, through which the fluid gurgles from its uncharted wellspring. In areas where cans of ichthyin have been found, careful study of the surroundings have revealed that such pipes full of spam are also hidden behind nearby walls. Several theories suggest that all molten meat stems from the same source, potentially an entire [[Level List|level]] which is overtaken with the dastardly substance, and that the presence of ichthyin is a harbinger of an orifice soon to be opened in a level's walls or ceiling. == Variants == === Foulfowl === '''Foulfowl''' is an uncommon variant of molten meat which appears to contain significantly lower amounts of myoglobin than spam. In terms of physical properties, this variant is remarkably similar to its more common counterpart; however, the effect which its gas has on a wanderer's mind is documented to be the exact opposite to that of spam. Instead of making the wanderer infatuated with it, foulfowl gas creates a strong aversion to the object in a wanderer and inclines them to escape its presence as quickly as possible. Research of this variant has only been possible due to protective equipment preventing this neurological phenomenon, which must be changed every few hours due to the gas's corrosive properties. === Ichthyin === '''Ichthyin''' is the rarest variant of molten meat. While it exudes the same cruoric gas as spam and boasts similar properties, this variant is unique in that it manifests not as pooling sludge gurgling from a massive orifice, but neatly packaged in cans made of a unique, gold-coloured metal, which are often labelled "SARDINES" in faded print. Despite their airtight appearance, cruoric gas is able to penetrate these cans and permeate the surrounding area, creating an environment similar to that which is produced by spam. This variant of molten meat is especially corrosive, and will instantly dissolve any substance which is not the metal can of its origin. Ichthyin typically has the smell of raw fish. == Notes ==
